description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly brownish-red coloration, interspersed with patches of gray and ochre. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring sharp edges, flat facets, and some weathered, pitted areas. One side of the rock displays a white label with handwritten text, suggesting it has been cataloged or studied, possibly for scientific or municipal purposes. The rock's coloration and texture indicate it is composed primarily of iron-rich stone, likely a type of sandstone or similar sedimentary rock. Its size and mass make it a substantial obstacle, suitable for use as a natural barrier or landscape feature in an urban environment. The overall appearance is that of a solid, heavy object with no visible cavities or openings.
description: A rugged, angular brownish-red rock with rough, uneven surfaces and a visible catalog label on one side. Its solid, heavy form makes it suitable as a natural barrier or landscape element.
description_view_0: A broad, multi-faceted face with prominent brown and gray coloration and visible rough texture.
description_view_1: Tall, narrow profile showing the white catalog label and a more vertical, slab-like surface.
description_view_2: Angled view highlighting the top and side surfaces, with the label partially visible and more pronounced color variation.
description_view_3: Side view showing a relatively flat, elongated face with less visible detail and no label.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
attribute_integration_with_urban_infrast: There is no visible evidence of intentional integration with urban infrastructure.

license_info.creator_display_name: UQ School of Earth and Environmental Sciences
